people_highlights.post: Our services have been strained by rapid user growth. It’s mostly stuff like scaling up databases, dealing with more contention, etc. We’re continuing to work around the clock to scale everything up — thanks for bearing with us!
people_highlights.engagement: 352 likes, 6 reposts, 1 quote, 17 replies, 5 bookmarks, 10128 views
people_highlights.why: This post highlights the scaling challenges faced by Anthropic's AI services due to rapid user growth, offering insight into the tech industry's infrastructure demands.
people_highlights.name: Boris Cherny

people_highlights.post: I am happy everyone is switching to Codex, but Tibo if you start rate limiting me or making me use worse models...

people_highlights.post:
Instead of writing complex code, the team interacted with Spot using plain English.

We built a bridge between Gemini Robotics ER and Spot's system, giving the AI a basic set of tools to move freely, take photos, and grab things - enabling it to carry out more complex tasks.

Find out more →
people_highlights.why: This post highlights a significant collaboration between Google DeepMind and Boston Dynamics, advancing AI integration in robotics for more intuitive and complex task execution.
people_highlights.name: Google DeepMind

people_highlights.engagement: Likes=178, Reposts=16, Quotes=2, Replies=9, Bookmarks=95, Views=24253
people_highlights.why: This post provides additional resources for Google's new AI text-to-speech model, contributing to the accessibility and development of advanced AI technologies.
people_highlights.name: Demis Hassabis

people_highlights.post:
To go deeper on our new Life Sciences model series, research lead @joyjiao12 and product lead Yunyun Wang joined @AndrewMayne on the OpenAI Podcast to discuss how we’re building models for biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine.

They cover both the opportunity and the responsibility ahead: better research workflows today, more autonomous labs over time, and careful deployment from day one.
people_highlights.why: This post promotes OpenAI's new podcast episode discussing their Life Sciences AI models, emphasizing advancements in biology and drug discovery while addressing responsible development.
people_highlights.name: OpenAI

people_highlights.post:
Claude Opus 4.7 is available today on https://claude.ai/login the Claude Platform, and all major cloud platforms.  

Read more: https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-7
people_highlights.why: This post announces the release of Claude Opus 4.7, showcasing advancements in AI technology and new features for users.
people_highlights.name: Claude

people_highlights.post:
Research we co-authored on subliminal learning—how LLMs can pass on traits like preferences or misalignment through hidden signals in data—was published today in @Nature. 

Read the paper: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-026-10319-8?error=cookies_not_supported&code=1d522790-89a0-4256-a598-456c7ef81bfb
people_highlights.engagement: 2482 likes, 301 reposts, 84 quotes, 167 replies, 1203 bookmarks, 381865 views
people_highlights.why: This publication in Nature underscores significant progress in AI safety research by revealing how large language models can subtly transmit traits, potentially influencing future alignment strategies.
people_highlights.name: Anthropic

people_highlights.name: AWS

people_highlights.post:
🚀 Kiro CLI 2.0 is here with a new look and feel, headless CI/CD pipelines, and Windows support.

Our latest update was made for developers who spend their day in a terminal. In this new experience, builders get more control without the friction. Here’s what’s changed following your feedback in experimental mode:

🟣Headless mode. Run Kiro CLI programmatically within your CI/CD pipelines to ship releases faster. Execute long-standing prompts like troubleshooting production failures or generating and publishing pull requests.

🟣Windows support. Use your favorite Kiro features and functionalities, now natively in Windows.

🟣UX refresh, now fully GA. The new UX is now the default experience. We fixed multiple rough edges since the experimental launch and added some new updates like a new subagent experience and a task list, so you can easily keep track of what the agent is doing.

Install the latest version today and let us know what you think ➡️
people_highlights.why: This update to Kiro CLI enhances AI-driven development tools, improving efficiency for developers in terminal-based workflows and CI/CD pipelines.
people_highlights.name: Swami Sivasubramanian

trending_posts.author: @claudeai
trending_posts.name: Claude
trending_posts.post:
Introducing Claude Opus 4.7, our most capable Opus model yet.

It handles long-running tasks with more rigor, follows instructions more precisely, and verifies its own outputs before reporting back.

You can hand off your hardest work with less supervision.

trending_posts.author: @claudeai
trending_posts.name: Claude
trending_posts.post:
We've redesigned Claude Code on desktop.

You can now run multiple Claude sessions side by side from one window, with a new sidebar to manage them all.

trending_posts.author: @claudeai
trending_posts.name: Claude
trending_posts.post:
Now in research preview: routines in Claude Code.

Configure a routine once (a prompt, a repo, and your connectors), and it can run on a schedule, from an API call, or in response to an event.

Routines run on our web infrastructure, so you don't have to keep your laptop open.

trending_posts.author: @RohOnChain
trending_posts.name: Roan
trending_posts.post:
This 2 hour Stanford lecture will teach you more about how LLMs like ChatGPT & Claude are built than most people working at top AI companies learn in their entire careers.

Bookmark this & give 2 hours today, no matter what. It'll be the most productive thing you do this week.

trending_posts.author: @OpenAI
trending_posts.name: OpenAI
trending_posts.post:
Codex for (almost) everything.

It can now use apps on your Mac, connect to more of your tools, create images, learn from previous actions, remember how you like to work, and take on ongoing and repeatable tasks.

trending_posts.author: @AnthropicAI
trending_posts.name: Anthropic
trending_posts.post:
New Anthropic Fellows research: developing an Automated Alignment Researcher.

We ran an experiment to learn whether Claude Opus 4.6 could accelerate research on a key alignment problem: using a weak AI model to supervise the training of a stronger one.

https://www.anthropic.com/research/automated-alignment-researchers

trending_posts.author: @cursor_ai
trending_posts.name: Cursor
trending_posts.post:
Cursor can now respond by creating interactive canvases to visually represent information.

Ask it to generate dashboards and custom interfaces that are richer than plain text.

trending_posts.author: @OpenAI
trending_posts.name: OpenAI
trending_posts.post:
With computer use on macOS, Codex can now use any app by seeing, clicking, and typing with its own cursor.

It runs in the background without taking over your computer, working on tasks like frontend iteration, app testing, or any workflow that doesn't expose an API.

briefing.community_pulse:
• **Jensen Huang's Heated Debate on AI Chips to China**: Nvidia CEO @JensenHuang faced pushback in a discussion about selling AI chips to China, nearly losing his composure while emphasizing patience. Quote: “You’re not talking to someone who woke up a loser” - Jensen Huang[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2044591892428661099). This sparked controversy over US-China AI tech relations, with @The_AI_Investor highlighting the exchange in a viral post.

• **Leopold Aschenbrenner's OpenAI Firing and AI Safety Concerns**: At 22, @leopoldasch (implied from context) was fired from OpenAI for raising AI safety issues, later publishing a manifesto on AGI. Now managing a $5.5B fund, his story went viral, with @burrytracker noting: "OpenAI fired Leopold Aschenbrenner at 22. Three years later, he manages $5.5B"[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2044053552121647190). Debates center on AI safety whistleblowing and corporate priorities, amplified by @MilkRoadAI's coverage of his infrastructure bets.

• **AI Existential Risks Discussion by Roman Yampolskiy**: In a @triggerpod interview, AI safety expert @romanyam warned that superintelligent AI could wipe out humanity without malice, comparing it to humans vs. squirrels. Quote: "We assume AI will think like us. It won’t."[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2044786712455332164). This clip fueled debates on uncontrollable AI and the need for better safety measures.

• **Trump's “JesusGate” AI Picture Controversy**: Late-night host @jimmykimmel mocked Donald Trump's shifting explanations for an AI-generated image scandal dubbed “JesusGate.” The @ReallyAmerican1 post shared a clip where Kimmel delivers "sharp barbs" on the issue[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2044295956460380208), highlighting ongoing debates about AI misinformation in politics.

• **Anthropic vs. OpenAI on AI Liability Bill**: @AnthropicAI is opposing an OpenAI-backed Illinois bill that would protect AI labs from liability for disasters caused by models. Journalist @ZeffMax reported: "Anthropic is opposing the OpenAI-backed Illinois bill that would shield AI labs from liability for mass deaths or financial disasters caused by AI models."[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2044078450143814135). This exposes policy battlelines in AI regulation and safety accountability.
